properties.descricao: DR Congo play their first World Cup 2026 game against Portugal on June 17 in Houston, but must isolate first.

properties.descricao: Observers describe a climate in which the chances of justice in the military tribunals are slim.

properties.titulo_evento: Western nations warn Israel to end illegal settlement expansion, violence
properties.descricao: Nine countries including UK, Germany and France warn settlements in occupied West Bank a 'breach of international law'.

properties.descricao: As petrol prices rise, new survey suggests economic confidence in the US is at -45, the worst since 2022.

properties.descricao: The dispute between players and the Grand Slams reaches boiling point in Paris, but boycott restricted to media duties.

properties.descricao: Poland's right-wing president has welcomed Trump's announcement.

properties.descricao: Kyiv denies Moscow's accusation that it hit a student dormitory, killing at least six people.

properties.descricao: Top Ukrainian officials claim Moscow's woes are growing as independent estimates suggest Russia's economy is faltering.

properties.titulo_evento: Russia's Putin vows retaliation after accusing Ukraine of hitting student dormitory
properties.descricao: Ukraine said it hit Russia's elite Rubicon drone military unit in a Moscow-occupied part of eastern Ukraine.

properties.titulo_evento: Rubio tries to reassure Nato allies over US troop deployments
properties.descricao: President Donald Trump says he wants to send more troops to Poland, a week after his officials cancelled a similar deployment.

properties.titulo_evento: Trump is putting pressure on Cuba - why and to what end?
properties.descricao: The US has warned that a peaceful agreement with Cuba is unlikely, Havana insists this is a "fraudulent case" designed to justify military intervention.

properties.titulo_evento: US navy chief says $14bn arms sale to Taiwan paused due to Iran war
properties.descricao: Hung Cao told a Senate hearing they were "doing a pause" on the sale to make sure they had munitions for the Iran war.

properties.descricao: The BBC speaks to Havana residents after the US charges the country's former leader, Raúl Castro, with murder.

properties.titulo_evento: From AI to interceptors, Ukraine is trying to drone-proof its skies
properties.descricao: After four years of Russia's full-scale invasion, Ukraine has become far more successful at fighting off air raids.

properties.descricao: The monumental structure was created by Parisan artist JR - often dubbed the French Banksy

properties.titulo_evento: US: Tulsi Gabbard resigns as Trump's intelligence chief
properties.descricao: Tulsi Gabbard has resigned as head of US intelligence, citing her husband's recent cancer diagnosis. An opponent of foreign US interventions, Gabbard has sought to distance herself from the US-Israeli war on Iran.

properties.titulo_evento: US or Iran: Who will win the Hormuz endurance game?
properties.descricao: Tehran is betting that its sanctions‑hardened economy can outlast the Strait of Hormuz crisis. Washington, meanwhile, is contending with elevated oil prices, renewed inflation pressure and rising global recession risks.

properties.titulo_evento: Iran war: 'Slight progress' in peace talks, Rubio says
properties.descricao: US Secretary of State Rubio said there was "a little bit of movement" in negotiations to end the Iran war. Meanwhile, four European nations called on Israel to stop the expansion of West Bank settlements. DW has more.

properties.titulo_evento: Germany news: Parliament approves reforms giving pharmacies more autonomy on vaccinations, prescriptions, tests
properties.descricao: Lawmakers passed a bill allowing pharmacies to issue certain prescription medications independently as well as to conduct more rapid tests. And bees besiege a Beetle in Düsseldorf's rush hour. DW has more.

properties.titulo_evento: New alliances emerge in Middle East — which one will 'win'?
properties.descricao: The UAE and Israel are getting closer, while Saudi Arabia is enhancing cooperation with Egypt, Turkey and Pakistan. The new alliances represent a rift in the Gulf, as approaches to the Iran war and the region diverge.

properties.titulo_evento: NATO chief welcomes Trump decision to send troops to Poland
properties.descricao: NATO foreign ministers are meeting in Sweden after Washington apparently reversed its plans to reduce its troop deployments to Poland. Meanwhile, Germany says defense spending will cross 4% of GDP.

properties.titulo_evento: Will Germany's aviation tax cut lower ticket prices?
properties.descricao: The tax cut aims to ease the burden on the aviation industry and make flying slightly cheaper. However, some experts warn that it is unlikely to have a meaningful impact on ticket prices.

properties.titulo_evento: Germany reaches out to Gulf states about Iran war
properties.descricao: Germany no longer believes it can play a mediating role in the conflict between the US, Israel and Iran. Instead, the German government is working to strengthen its ties with the Gulf states.

properties.titulo_evento: Merz, Meloni and allies castigate Israel over West Bank settler violence
properties.descricao: “The policies and practices of the Israeli government, including a further entrenchment of Israeli control, are undermining stability and prospects for a two-state solution,” top European leaders warned.

properties.titulo_evento: Russia’s drone blame game fails to split Ukraine from its Baltic allies
properties.descricao: Despite stray Ukrainian drones crossing into NATO airspace, Kyiv's allies are standing firm.

properties.titulo_evento: Top EU prosecutor complains to Brussels about Greece amid farm fraud probe
properties.descricao: The Greek government earlier this week submitted an amendment to effectively block European prosecutors from probing lawmakers.

properties.titulo_evento: Tony Blair’s consultancy doubles down on AI and restructures in Europe
properties.descricao: The move will affect its recently opened Brussels office.
